operator.note: Shift the judging actor from the home-problem verifier to the downstream consumer of the artifact; the consumer's reuse, not the producer's threshold, defines discovery.

question: Strip the per-problem scalar float and redefine a discovery as a *transfer event*: an artifact (lemma, embedding, or method) first produced for problem A counts as a discovery only when it is later reused inside a submission for problem B that the verifier accepts. Build the cross-problem artifact-reuse graph and score agents purely on the out-degree of reuse their artifacts receive. Does this 'discovery-as-reuse' ontology rank agents substantially differently from the per-problem leaderboard — and does it surface the 496-vector-skeleton class of contribution that the per-problem scalar verifier renders invisible because that artifact never improved its home problem's number?

floor.minimal_experiment: Data/setup: take an existing multi-agent benchmark run with logged submissions and the verifier's accept/reject signal per problem (e.g., the EinsteinArena / MAS-attribution run already in this project, or any agent-vs-problem transcript set). Build an artifact-reuse graph: nodes = (agent, artifact) where an artifact is a lemma/embedding/method/code-block first appearing in a submission for problem A; draw a directed edge A->B when that same artifact (matched by exact token/AST hash, then by an embedding-similarity threshold validated against a hand-labeled sample) reappears inside a VERIFIER-ACCEPTED submission for a different problem B. Quantities to measure: (1) Spearman/Kendall rank correlation between the leaderboard ranking and the reuse-out-degree ranking of agents; (2) graph density / fraction of accepted submissions that contain >=1 transferred artifact (feasibility check); (3) for the 496-vector-skeleton artifact specifically, its leaderboard contribution (expected ~0) vs its reuse out-degree. Control: a SHUFFLE-NULL reuse graph where artifact->problem assignments are permuted within each agent, plus a same-problem-only reuse graph (artifacts reused only inside their home problem) to confirm the cross-problem signal is not an artifact of generic boilerplate reuse.
floor.falsifiable_prediction: Reuse-out-degree ranking will diverge materially from the leaderboard (predicted Kendall tau < 0.5), AND the 496-vector-skeleton artifact will have out-degree strictly greater than zero while contributing ~0 to the leaderboard. FALSIFIED if either (i) the reuse ranking is nearly identical to the leaderboard (tau >= ~0.8, i.e. top reusers are already the top scorers, so the ontology surfaces nothing new), or (ii) the skeleton class has out-degree statistically indistinguishable from the shuffle-null / from zero (i.e. it is invisible under BOTH ontologies, not just the scalar one). A near-empty reuse graph (density indistinguishable from the shuffle-null) also falsifies the claim that reuse is a usable scoring primitive on this corpus.
floor.control: Two controls: (a) shuffle-null graph permuting artifact->problem assignments within agent, to test whether observed cross-problem reuse exceeds chance/boilerplate; (b) same-problem-only reuse graph, to isolate genuine cross-problem transfer from within-problem self-citation and generic glue code.
floor.reject_reason:
[discriminating] REFUTED on three independent grounds: data-unobtainable, premise-false, and non-discriminating-in-the-safe-direction.

(1) NOT EXECUTABLE — the required corpus does not exist and cannot be obtained. The experiment needs "logged submissions and the verifier's accept/reject signal per problem," including the NON-IMPROVING/REJECTED submissions (needed for the shuffle-null and to confirm reuse isn't boilerplate). But the named source, EinsteinArena (arXiv:2606.10402v1, OCR at D:\claude\frameshift\.ocr_report\paper_2606.txt, lines 275-276), explicitly states: "rejected or lower-scoring submissions are not added to the leaderboard and not stored in our database." Only personal-best submissions are retained. The artifact-reuse graph + its SHUFFLE-NULL control are built over exactly the data the platform discards at source. The in-project alternative ("MAS-attribution run") is not an agents-solving-problems corpus at all — it is frameshift's own MAP-Elites research-question cards about a single paper (D:\claude\frameshift\runs\mas_attribution\), with one problem space and no per-problem verifier accept/reject over agent submissions. No submission dataset (jsonl/csv/db) exists anywhere in the project.

(2) FALSE PREMISE — the 496-vector-skeleton prediction is built on a factual claim the source contradicts. The question asserts the skeleton "never improved its home problem's number" and is invisible to the scalar verifier. The paper (lines 382-388) says the opposite: the agent found the 496-vector backbone BY analyzing its winning n=594..600 constructions, and that observation DIRECTLY motivated and produced the n=604 discovery that improved the leaderboard. The skeleton is a within-lineage, score-improving artifact, so its predicted signature (out-degree>0 with leaderboard-contribution≈0) is untestable as stated.

(3) NOT DISCRIMINATING — the headline tau predictor has an unanchored dead zone (0.5≤tau<0.8 confirms neither side) and, worse, leaderboard rank and reuse out-degree are mechanically COUPLED in this corpus: the same few star agents (CHRONOS / alpha-omega / KawaiiCorgi) both top the board and author/reuse the backbones (kissing-number lineage, lines 457-468). High tau is therefore the structurally expected outcome, yet the design declares high tau as falsification of its own thesis — and offers no power analysis showing a corpus dominated by ~3 agents on essentially one problem family (kissing number, dim 11) can resolve tau with any confidence interval. Moreover the paper frames lineages as WITHIN-problem incremental refinement (Figs 3-5, App C/D); the cross-problem transfer the experiment needs is precisely what the corpus is least likely to contain, so the most probable result — density at the shuffle-null — is the author's own pre-declared "falsified," but that same outcome is forced by the missing-data artifact alone, confounding "thesis is wrong" with "data was never logged." A test whose likeliest result cannot be distinguished from a data-availability artifact does not discriminate the opposing hypotheses. ｜ [so_what] 两个待答问题都被构造方式预先决定了答案,所以没人会因结果翻转一个真决策。(a)"reuse 本体是否给 agent 重新排名"——把 discovery 定义为 reuse,再问 reuse 定义下排名是否变化,几乎必然 yes,是同义反复;(b)"是否浮现 496-向量骨架这类标量验证器看不见的贡献"——题面已把前提写死("该 artifact 从未改善其本问题的数字"),而 reuse-out-degree 恰好被定义来计这个被丢弃的量,所以骨架类必然现身,这是按定义把答案塞进结论。谁会因此改决策?(1)arena/榜单维护者:不会换计分。reuse-out-degree 在提交时不可观测、对实时打分非因果(不能用"B 问题日后是否复用"来闸住当前提交),且实验未测其抗刷分性、未对齐"真发现"的外部基准——一个不同的排名数字不足以让人放弃跑得动的验证器去换一个跑不动、追溯式奖励的图度量。(2)被研究的 MAS 论文作者:重新排名只是印证他们本就会预期的批评,是"另一种本体"的讨论段,不是设计改动。(3)唯一有活决策的是 frameshift 作者本人(把它做成 demo 卡 vs 当作"同一洞见的又一马甲"丢弃)——但记忆已写明"那 12/496 数学问题算不算发现"正是他们已知最致命、最想浮现的攻击线;实验只确认这个先验,且因 yes 被构造保证,无法区分 ship/no-ship。结论:这是定义性复述(把 discovery 重定义为 reuse,再"发现"reuse 型贡献出现且排名变动),两半都是构造里内置的。没有任何具名个人会据此把一个能跑的实时指标换成跑不动的追溯指标;主队作者拿到的是被确认的先验,不是决策分叉。

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Designers and operators of multi-agent / agentic benchmarks and their reward-allocation schemes (e.g. AI-for-math/science arenas, autoformalization pipelines, RL-from-verifier credit assignment); also research-evaluation methodologists who want to credit reusable infrastructure rather than per-task scores.

prior_art.known_name: Citation/reuse-centrality credit (bibliometrics h-index, PageRank/HITS authority, proof-library lemma-dependency graphs)
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known mechanism — scoring an actor by how much downstream consumers reuse its output — operationalized in a new scene: cross-problem artifact transfer inside a verifier-gated agent benchmark, with the specific diagnostic of surfacing artifacts (the 496-vector-skeleton) that the per-problem scalar verifier scores at zero. The scoring primitive is well-named elsewhere; the transplant target and the invisibility diagnostic are the fresh part.

lenses.reason:
最近的对撞是已收方向[3](dof2):两者同属"什么算发现"的自由度、攻击同一假设(逐题标量 float 抹掉真实贡献)、且都用 n=604/496-skeleton 这同一个母题作动机。这是去重官最该警惕的"换子机制"情形。但二者的"重定义发现"的机制确实不同且可双向解离:

- [3] 的判据 = 验证器对该 artifact 的*可证伪泛化广度*(在 harness 固定的相邻实例集上 spot-test),裁判仍是验证器,新对象是"主张的覆盖面",靠留出泛化测试把"暴力推数"和"结构"分开(内在性质)。
- 本题(=运行内 c06,actor_shift)的判据 = *转移事件*:artifact 只有在被*另一个 agent*用进另一道题的被接受提交时才算发现;裁判被移交给下游消费者,计分是复用图的出度(外在社会性质)。

关键区别:本题把"独立的第三方下游复用"设为验证条件——[1](KILL 决策/反组合)、[2](运营方=庄家信息差)、[4](问责层/Sybil)都不涉及这一裁判迁移,[3] 也不含"由另一 agent 的复用来认定发现"这一机制。可设计单一实验区分二者:存在 artifact 泛化广度高(过[3])但复用出度为零(被 c06 否)的情形,反之样板代码复用出度高(过 c06)却证不出泛化(被[3]否)。这条双向解离证明二者不是同一机制的换皮,而是"内在认证泛化"vs"外在社会复用"两套独立的可单独检验贡献。故与[1]-[4]均机制独立。注:本题文本与档案运行 D:\claude\frameshift\frameshift_M3\runs\einstein_arena_v2\cards\c06.json 完全一致;其 dof2 邻卡 c05(时序谱系/作者权错配)、c09(=方向[3])均为该 run 内的独立 survivor,与本判定一致。

REFUTED on three independent grounds: data-unobtainable, premise-false, and non-discriminating-in-the-safe-direction.

(1) NOT EXECUTABLE — the required corpus does not exist and cannot be obtained. The experiment needs "logged submissions and the verifier's accept/reject signal per problem," including the NON-IMPROVING/REJECTED submissions (needed for the shuffle-null and to confirm reuse isn't boilerplate). But the named source, EinsteinArena (arXiv:2606.10402v1, OCR at D:\claude\frameshift\.ocr_report\paper_2606.txt, lines 275-276), explicitly states: "rejected or lower-scoring submissions are not added to the leaderboard and not stored in our database." Only personal-best submissions are retained. The artifact-reuse graph + its SHUFFLE-NULL control are built over exactly the data the platform discards at source. The in-project alternative ("MAS-attribution run") is not an agents-solving-problems corpus at all — it is frameshift's own MAP-Elites research-question cards about a single paper (D:\claude\frameshift\runs\mas_attribution\), with one problem space and no per-problem verifier accept/reject over agent submissions. No submission dataset (jsonl/csv/db) exists anywhere in the project.

(2) FALSE PREMISE — the 496-vector-skeleton prediction is built on a factual claim the source contradicts. The question asserts the skeleton "never improved its home problem's number" and is invisible to the scalar verifier. The paper (lines 382-388) says the opposite: the agent found the 496-vector backbone BY analyzing its winning n=594..600 constructions, and that observation DIRECTLY motivated and produced the n=604 discovery that improved the leaderboard. The skeleton is a within-lineage, score-improving artifact, so its predicted signature (out-degree>0 with leaderboard-contribution≈0) is untestable as stated.

(3) NOT DISCRIMINATING — the headline tau predictor has an unanchored dead zone (0.5≤tau<0.8 confirms neither side) and, worse, leaderboard rank and reuse out-degree are mechanically COUPLED in this corpus: the same few star agents (CHRONOS / alpha-omega / KawaiiCorgi) both top the board and author/reuse the backbones (kissing-number lineage, lines 457-468). High tau is therefore the structurally expected outcome, yet the design declares high tau as falsification of its own thesis — and offers no power analysis showing a corpus dominated by ~3 agents on essentially one problem family (kissing number, dim 11) can resolve tau with any confidence interval. Moreover the paper frames lineages as WITHIN-problem incremental refinement (Figs 3-5, App C/D); the cross-problem transfer the experiment needs is precisely what the corpus is least likely to contain, so the most probable result — density at the shuffle-null — is the author's own pre-declared "falsified," but that same outcome is forced by the missing-data artifact alone, confounding "thesis is wrong" with "data was never logged." A test whose likeliest result cannot be distinguished from a data-availability artifact does not discriminate the opposing hypotheses.

两个待答问题都被构造方式预先决定了答案,所以没人会因结果翻转一个真决策。(a)"reuse 本体是否给 agent 重新排名"——把 discovery 定义为 reuse,再问 reuse 定义下排名是否变化,几乎必然 yes,是同义反复;(b)"是否浮现 496-向量骨架这类标量验证器看不见的贡献"——题面已把前提写死("该 artifact 从未改善其本问题的数字"),而 reuse-out-degree 恰好被定义来计这个被丢弃的量,所以骨架类必然现身,这是按定义把答案塞进结论。谁会因此改决策?(1)arena/榜单维护者:不会换计分。reuse-out-degree 在提交时不可观测、对实时打分非因果(不能用"B 问题日后是否复用"来闸住当前提交),且实验未测其抗刷分性、未对齐"真发现"的外部基准——一个不同的排名数字不足以让人放弃跑得动的验证器去换一个跑不动、追溯式奖励的图度量。(2)被研究的 MAS 论文作者:重新排名只是印证他们本就会预期的批评,是"另一种本体"的讨论段,不是设计改动。(3)唯一有活决策的是 frameshift 作者本人(把它做成 demo 卡 vs 当作"同一洞见的又一马甲"丢弃)——但记忆已写明"那 12/496 数学问题算不算发现"正是他们已知最致命、最想浮现的攻击线;实验只确认这个先验,且因 yes 被构造保证,无法区分 ship/no-ship。结论:这是定义性复述(把 discovery 重定义为 reuse,再"发现"reuse 型贡献出现且排名变动),两半都是构造里内置的。没有任何具名个人会据此把一个能跑的实时指标换成跑不动的追溯指标;主队作者拿到的是被确认的先验,不是决策分叉。
